steps.title: Find the singular subject
steps.content: The subject is the singular noun "analysis." The names James Melton and Tom Ginsburg show possession, and the phrases after "of" add description, but they do not change the subject's number.

steps.title: Reject plural verbs
steps.content:
• Choice A uses the plural verb phrase "are identifying."
  • Choice B uses the plural present-tense verb "identify."
  • Choice C uses the plural helping verb "have."
The remaining choice is the singular present-tense verb that agrees with "analysis." The answer is D.
